mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or directional accuracy falls below 48% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates resilient manufacturing output that offsets monetary tightening pressures, creating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above the 255 threshold signal entrenched inflation that forces central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above threshold correlates with positive forward returns in >50% of observations, or inflation expectations fail to adjust equity pricing models.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no predictive power over 60-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses direction during labor market tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that directly supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns that trigger short-term risk aversion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO shocks fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 48% in walk-forward tests over any 3-year period.
